Assembly: The assembly ilNemBetu1.hap1.1 is based on 176x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was identified by BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements~(https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4) and read coverageNo Chromosome W was identified at this time.The order and orientation of scaffolds in the following locations is uncertain:Chromosome 23 between approximately 2.26-2.83MbChromosome 28 between approximately 3.97-4.22Mb.

Assembly: This project provides the diploid genome assembly bLusSve1 of the Bluethroat. The sample was provided by Arild Johnsen, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bLusSve1 is based on 30x PromethION R10 Oxford Nanopore data and 55x Arima Hi-C data. The R10 ONT reads were assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size. Both sex chromosomes have been added to hap1.

Assembly: The assembly daConArve2.hap1.1 is based on 32x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Heterozygous inversions were observed at the following locations: chromosome 1, breakpoints at 17.9 Mb and 48.8 Mb; chromosome 3, breakpoints at 8.7 Mb, 10.4 Mb, 21.7 Mb and 23.9 mb; chromosome 15, breakpoints at 20.3 Mb and 35.4 Mb; chromosome 20, breakpoints at 9.5 Mb, 11.6 Mb, 29.6 Mb and 31.7 Mb. The order and orientation of contigs is uncertain in the following regions: chromosome 4, 39 Mb to 40.7 Mb; chromosome 5, 36.5 Mb to 39.9 Mb; chromosome 20, 24 Mb to 26.1 Mb; chromosome 22, 26.5 Mb to 28 mb; chromosome 23, 17.3 Mb to 19.2 Mb; chromosome 24, 25.7 Mb to 29 Mb.

Assembly: The assembly ilChoDian1.hap1.1 is based on 93x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based on BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements ( https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).The Z and W chromosomes were identified based on its single-copy status within a diploid assembly. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome W are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 15 (7,200 - 7,927 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 25 (2,639 - 3,094 Kbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly of Tripterygion delaisi (fTriDel1) is based on 52X PacBio data and Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr).The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 20 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 1.1 Mb (with the largest being 248Kb). Additionally, 249 regions totaling 9.6Mb (with the largest being 700 Kb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptgaul. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: Primary assembly. Maternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the maternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data. This assembly was then upraded by employing in silico chromosome painting of the draft scaffolds in the assembly against the American Porcupine (EreDor1) assembly and against the NMR FISH-Karyoptype published in Romanenko et al., 2023 (PMID: 380307020). This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres. Ambiguous syntenic alignments were resolved by inspecting our Hi-C-seq data at the end points of those regions. Chromosome names were identified in collaboration with GeneCodehc.

Assembly: Primary assembly. paternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the paternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data. This assembly was then upraded by employing in silico chromosome painting of the draft scaffolds in the assembly against the American Porcupine (EreDor1) assembly and against the NMR FISH-Karyoptype published in Romanenko et al., 2023 (PMID: 380307020). This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres.

Assembly: The assembly ilAchFlav2.1 is based on 49x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based on synteny with Habrosyne pyritoides (GCA_907165245.1; ilHabPyri1).

Assembly: The assembly daSolDulc1.2 is based on 27x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative circular sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Uncertain order and orientation of scaffolds in repetitive regions on chromosome 6 (15.6-16.5 Mbp). Chromosome 10 shows an inversion between haplotypes (41 - 44 Mbp).

Assembly: Paternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the paternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data.

Assembly: Primary assembly. Maternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the maternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed using trio-binning, the Flye aligner, and polishing, 10X-scaffolding, and Hi-C scaffolding piplines established in both the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data.

Assembly: The assembly fAciRut3.2 is based on 61x PacBio data and Dovetail Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: HiFi reads were trio-binned using trioCanu with k-mers from the Illumina-sequenced parents; haplotype-specific HiFi reads were assembled separately using Hifiasm; segregation of HiC reads using Canu's 'splitHaplotype' script and k-mers from the Illumina-sequenced parents; for each haplotype assembly, haplotype-specific and unclassified HiC reads were combined to construct scaffolds using pins. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilCatAlch1.hap1.1 is based on 116x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly and by HiC signal. The Z chromosome was also identified based on BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). On Chromosome W, from 0 bp to 3,677 Kbp and from 8,963 Kbp to 27,149 Kbp, the order and orientation is uncertain.

Assembly: The assembly of Acanthurus tractus (fAcaTra1) is based on 52X PacBio data and 194X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. No contigs were found to be contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral). Additionally, 35 regions totaling 1.801 Mb (with the largest being 0.223 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. We note a difference in the number of chromosomes for this species (16 chromosomes) compared to the number of chromosomes in the species Acanthurus coeruleus (fAcaCoe1), which has 24.

Assembly: The assembly of Muraena helena (fMurHel1) is based on 42X PacBio data and 203X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr).The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, no contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial,viral,archae). Additionally, 191 regions totaling 85 Mb (with the largest being 8.2 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using oatk and the sequence was linear. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, no supplementary haplotypic regions were removed. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.
